title: Train a monotone-constrained booster on physically directional features
summary: Several engineered features should be monotone in pit propensity under fixed context, such as service pressure, finish-stop pressure, and impossibility of finishing on current tyres.
rationale: Several engineered features should be monotone in pit propensity under fixed context, such as service pressure, finish-stop pressure, and impossibility of finishing on current tyres. XGBoost supports explicit monotonic constraints, which can reduce brittle tree interactions and improve generalization when hand-built strategy features are already strong.
implementation_hint: Select a compact feature subset with strong physical directionality and fit an XGBoost model with signed monotone constraints. Blend it with the unconstrained main model rather than replacing it; test whether it improves OOF-to-public stability more than raw CV.
expected_effect: Likely modest CV gain but potentially better leaderboard transfer by regularizing pathological splits around sparse race/driver regimes.
risk: Wrong constraint signs or overly broad constrained feature sets can make trees too shallow and suppress useful interactions.
